entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Launchpad Extension v3.2 – Dark Mode, Auto-Login & More
entry.content:
What is the SAP Cloud ERP Launchpad Extension?

 

A quick recap for new readers: this extension (available for Chrome, Edge, and Firefox) lets you save your favourite SAP Fiori apps and Cloud ERP system URLs directly in the browser toolbar. Instead of navigating through bookmarks or typing URLs manually, you open the extension popup, click your app, and you're there. Read more here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/can-a-free-chrome-extension-transform-your-sap-cloud-erp-experience/ba-p/13801414

 

🌙 Dark Mode

The most visually impactful change: the extension now supports a full dark mode, consistent across both the popup and the options page.

To switch it on, open the Options page (click the extension icon → settings, or right-click → "Extension options") and toggle the Dark Mode switch in the footer bar.

 

The dark theme applies to:

- The extension popup (tree view, buttons, footer bar, search field)

- The full Options page (tables, dialogs, tab headers, the shell bar)

Your preference is saved automatically and persists across browser restarts.

 

🔑 Auto-Login with Saved Credentials

You can now save a username and password for each of your Cloud ERP systems. When you navigate to a saved system's SAP Fiori Launchpad for the first time, the extension automatically fills in the login form for you — no typing needed.

How to save credentials

  1. Open the Options page
  2. Go to the Cloud ERP Systems tab
  3. Click Create to add a new system, or select an existing one and click Edit
  4. Fill in the optional Username and Password fields
  5. Click Save

 

The password is stored securely in your browser's sync storage and is shown as "Saved" in the table — the actual value is never displayed again for security.

How auto-login works

 

Once credentials are saved, the extension's background service will detect when you open a matching SAP system URL and automatically inject your username and password into the login form. This works seamlessly in the background — you just arrive at the Fiori Launchpad already logged in.

 

> Note: Credentials are stored only locally in your browser and are never transmitted anywhere. When you export your Cloud ERP Systems list, the download file contains only the name and host URL — credentials are intentionally excluded to protect your privacy.

 

🏢Adding Cloud ERP Systems with Login Credentials Directly from the Popup

The popup now includes Username and Password fields when adding a Cloud ERP System through the Add → Cloud ERP System menu.

 

🔒 Privacy-Safe Export

When you download your Cloud ERP Systems list (Download button in the footer of the Options page, while on the Cloud ERP Systems tab), the exported JSON file now contains only the name and host URL for each system.

Usernames and passwords are never included in exports. This means you can safely shareyour system list with colleagues without worrying about credential exposure.

 

🦊 Firefox: Fixes and Improvements

Several improvements have been made specifically for Firefox users:

- Tree duplication fixed: A Firefox-specific bug caused the Fiori Apps tree in the popup to render items twice. This was caused by Firefox firing browser storage callbacks more than once under certain conditions. The issue has been fully resolved.

- Upload fixed: The file upload (importing JSON) now works correctly in Firefox.

- Translations: All new field labels (Username column, Password column, "Saved" status) are now fully translated across all 18 supported languages in the Firefox version.

 

🔗Download and Install

Chrome:

https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/sap-cloud-erp-launchpad/nelmoakcfgfgkigcjgkmibhmgfpbhcbh

Edge:

https://microsoftedge.microsoft.com/addons/detail/fiori-launchpad-for-sap-c/kfbfaeokdhfbeikhofemebocbbfcmpcb

Firefox:

https://addons.mozilla.org/de/firefox/addon/sap-cloud-erp-launchpad/

 

Documentation:

https://edrilanberisha.github.io/sapclouderplaunchpad/

 

💬 Feedback Welcome

 

As always, this extension is built for the SAP community. If you find bugs, have feature requests, or just want to say the dark mode made your day — please leave a comment below or open an issue on GitHub.

 

Happy navigating! 🚀

entry.title: Re: The SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Integration Roadmap
entry.content: We got that same message, not idea what it is about but would be interested to know more

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Columbus - Ohio, July 25th, 2026
entry.content:
First time in Columbus

We are organizing SAP INSIDE TRACK in Columbus, Ohio, for the first time. The main reason for organizing this event is to continue our community awareness in the Ohio State area; hopefully, we can grow it into other cities and perhaps other states.

If you like to learn and network, and if you work in S/4HANA, BTP, AI, Fiori, functional and technical sides or any other SAP area, then this event is for you. We will also have food and drinks at the event as well. Don't miss the opportunity to participate. See you there.


Why Attend?

Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just beginning your SAP journey, SAP Inside Track Columbus offers something for everyone:

  • Learn from SAP experts, practitioners, and community leaders
  • Explore the latest developments across the SAP ecosystem
  • Discover real-world customer stories and implementation experiences
  • Network with SAP professionals from across the Midwest region
  • Engage in discussions around emerging technologies and best practices
  • Meet fellow community members and expand your professional network

Agenda

Time (in EST)SpeakerDescription
9:00 AM SIT Columbus Logo and Tagline unveil
9:15 AMPawan KhatriERP Implementation scope in USA
10:15 AMManish GargSCM in Pharma Industry
11:00 AM Tea and Snacks Break
11:15 AMVaibhav MishraIntegrating AI-Based Predictive Maintenance with SAP S4HANA
12:15 PMLeela Vinod CudapahFrom Consultants to AI-Orchestrators: Transforming SAP Implementation Ways of Working for the AI Era
1:00 PM Lunch (sponsored by eBusiness Solutions Inc.)
2:00 PMPriyanka MishraPhase-based Maintenance in SAP S4HANA Asset Management. - Challenges and Case Studies
3:00 PMRajiv ShahSAP FICO in SAP S4HANA


Our Sponsor
This event is sponsored by eBusiness Solutions Inc. eBusiness Solutions provided the meeting rooms, breakout area, and lunch for this event. 

Location

200 E Campus View Blvd STE 200, Columbus, OH 43235

Parking
Free parking is available at the location. We can easily accommodate more than 30 four-wheelers.

Globally
The event is held in several countries around the world. Its organizers are active members of the SAP Community to share knowledge and strengthen professionals among the participants.
The event has been held in several cities around the world for over 15 years.

Learn more about how to hold your SAP Inside Track event in your city through this LINK.

Wei

entry.title: Error : Unknown Account Assignment not defined for use here
entry.content:
Hi, During the creation of Release order with reference to contract using BAPI PO Create1 the error : 'Unknown Account Assignment not defined for use here' is displayed. 

The contract is with AAC ( Account Assignment category) -U. During creation of release order the AAC is changed from U to K and the other mandatory field values GL code and Cost Center is entered. However the error is displayed.

entry.title: the inspection of Intercompany Process for Customer Returns (2F4) can be automated processing
entry.content:
Hi,

In the Intercompany Process for Customer Returns, I have to use the scope item 2F4. 

When I post the Returns Delivery , the system will create the inspection document , I have to confirm the inspection document .

I need to receive inventory, but do not require quality inspection confirmation on the system.

The inspection document can be setted to skip ?  Or are  there development interfaces available for automated processing ?

entry.title: Joule Work on RISE — do SAP GUI users need to move to Fiori, or only for the embedded Joule Assistan
entry.content:
We're a RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition) customer, and most users still work in classic SAP GUI, not Fiori.

My understanding is that the embedded Joule Assistant is surfaced inside the UI, so that would require Fiori — but Joule Work is a separate, standalone agentic workspace that executes against the backend via
APIs, independent of the user's transactional screen.

So my question is: for Joule Work specifically, can our SAP GUI users keep using GUI as-is, or do they need to migrate to Fiori?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite Service Plan Issue
entry.content:
Per your description, you have different service plans:

  • Prod: Standard
  • Test: successfactors_edition

There are some features available since higher plans like Standard, and they can only be available when you upgrade your Test to a higher service plan.

entry.title: S4HC | Batch Check Printing
entry.content:
Hi Sir/Ma'am,

Hi Sir/Ma'am,

We would like to ask for your assistance on what scope items are needed to be activated for Batch Check Printing if this is standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, as well as any documentation, if ever.

Thank you.

 

Sincerely,

Melvin Banggo

entry.title: Aging of Revenue / VAT
entry.content:
Hi Sir/Ma'am,

Our client has this specific requirement and we want to ask for your assistance if there is standard functionality available for this in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and what are the workarounds, if ever.

Requirement:

Instead of the usual aging of the receivables, the client also age the VAT and Revenue lines of the sales transaction.  They generate reports based on which VAT and revenue accounts/lines are still outstanding and how old are they.

We greatly appreciate your assistance on this concern.

 

Thank you very much and God bless.

Sincerely,


Melvin Banggo

entry.title: Re: Worflow inbox - Error when starting submethod
entry.content: The solution was described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/error-when-starting-submethod/qaa-p/14426436/highlight/true#M4944672

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content:
Greetings,

check if you have additional objects assigned to your maintenance plan item. Use transaction IP03. You will find maintenance plan number in planning tab of your maintenance order. 

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Partner user cannot see Partnership section in Sap4Me
entry.content: In order to get the full benefits of SAP for Me, ensure that you contact your Partner User Administrator (previously called Partner Security Manager) to provide you with the permissions you need for your specific role. If you do not know who is your Partner User Administrator, just check SAP for Me > My Important Contacts To get further help, you may refer to the attachment to use get support channel to contact the responsible partner support team. Please also see this KBA for more info: 3165784 - How to get assistance for Partner Dashboards and the related partner cards - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehous...
entry.content:
Hi @KGANTI2022 ,

creation of Warhouse Task  as follow up inside /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST is a common way. Inside the Badi Implementation you should call qRFC  for example /SCWM/TO_CREATE_MOVE_HU with prefix WMTH.

entry.title: Re: FSCM - Credit Limit Requests Approval using Manage Credit Management Rules
entry.content: @darrelzaulda Dear, I have tried to follow the steps you shared as above, but after the manage situation types, the approvers could not receive the email notification, do you know where i need to check, thanks.

entry.title: Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standard role for role cat. MKK
entry.content:
When creating a sales order using the Domestic Account Group (ZDOM), I receive the following error:

"Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standard role for role category MKK."

However, when I create a sales order using the Foreign Account Group (ZFRN), the sales order is created successfully without any issues.

I have already compared the backend configuration for both account groups, and the configuration appears to be the same. Despite this, the error occurs only for ZDOM.

Could you please help identify what might be causing this issue or advise which additional configuration or master data should be checked?

entry.title: Re: Recording one characteristic at multiple fixed measur...
entry.content:
Greetings,

have you tried using a different sampling procedure for the "special" characteristics? In example below for the first two characteristcs only one result is required and fo the last one 5 results need to be entered. This is managed by sampling procedure which is assigned to inspection characteristic on the ionspection plan.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content:
Well, it's the end of the month, which means time for a wrap up of the Developer Challenge we've just had. It's been a lot of fun for us to create the questions and see how much you've all participated, and, from your comments, how much you've also enjoyed it. Thank you for taking part!

As you know, there are no prizes, the only reward is increased knowledge and understanding of a fundamentally important technology in the SAP developer universe. We'd love to hear from you (in comments to this post) what you thought, how you found the questions, whether you'd like to see something this again, and anything else that occurs to you.

Half way through the month, we published a brief analysis of your progress and success on the questions so far, and continued to update that on a daily basis. We've just run a final analysis update today (Sunday 28 June), which should have given you all enough time to answer the final question which was published at the beginning of last Friday.

 

Facts, figures and heroes

Over the 20 questions you all submitted a total of 753 responses, which is fab! Thank you to everyone who did submit at least one response.

Bronze

In fact, here's a list of what we're going to call "Bronze" winners - everyone who participated:

AakankshaWs AfshanAnsari Andrii Anusha2025 Caisi Chaimae_Chairi27 Dan_Wroblewski FlorianP1 GauravPhadnis Greltel Guru_Charan HarshPatelV Henning Ihor_Haranichev JFenris Jawaharch Joery Laco M-Uzi MatLakaemper MattHarding Mayank_Mittal8 MioYasutake MohamedAbdelghany NakulG NeelimaEmmani NiklausB PVSBPrasadK PrabhuDC RajeshOlive Raphael Rupesh_Kumar25 SSaiSanthosh SachinArtani SergioG_TX Sushakude Swathi_SAP_Philomath SyamsundarKarukola VishnAndr YLN Yogananda _Jakub_ aelghannam ajmaradiaga ankitsrivastav aryan-11 berkaydurmaz berkayergun cayotteRizing ditt_kalyan furkan5 hrushikeshraju_yerram juancarlos_dasilva kasch-code lidavidlee mhameister milan_10 mvaibhav nagarjundas_1994 omer_sakar parthasaradhi sarvesh_agarwal satya01 shotokka shubhamkar soheil_st soisses stickman_0x00 sudarshan_b tchaitanya tobiasz_h

(Sorry we're not able to tag you all, the blogging platform limits the number of users one can mention in a single post to just 20).

Silver

There's a special subset of these participants who provided a response to every single question, well done and thank you for your perseverance and tenacity!

- @SachinArtani
- @shotokka
- @kasch-code
- @Swathi_SAP_Philomath
- @MatLakaemper
- @parthasaradhi
- @Dan_Wroblewski
- @Laco
- @ajmaradiaga
- @Anusha2025
- @VishnAndr
- @Joery
- @lidavidlee
- @Andrii
- @MioYasutake
- @berkaydurmaz
- @mvaibhav
- @Rupesh_Kumar25
- @furkan5

Gold

And there's an elite subset of the silver subset who not only provided a response to every single question, but got them all correct, amazing work, thank you and well done indeed! 🚀

- @SachinArtani
- @shotokka
- @kasch-code
- @MatLakaemper
- @parthasaradhi
- @Dan_Wroblewski
- @mvaibhav
- @furkan5

Wrapping up

Well, that just about wraps it up for this month's Developer Challenge. We hope you had fun, and learned something. Let us know also in the comments what you think of the CAP documentation that you've been exploring and studying over the last few weeks. We (and the CAP documentation folks) would love to know.

We'll keep the results OData v4 service online and available for the next few days, in case you want to do some analysis of your own. 

Thanks everyone!

entry.title: The status of item category SVS4 in the Service Order cannot be set to "Completed".
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

I use Service Order in the Public Cloud.
Material A is registered with the item category group SVBL, and Material B with SRVM.
I registered the service order as follows.

ItemParentServiceOrderItemproductitem categorystatus
10 ASVB4Released
2010BSVS4Released
     

When the Service Order is released, a Purchase Requisition is created for Item 20.
Subsequently, a Purchase Order was created and a Goods Receipt was posted.

When I changed the status in the Service Order header to "Completed," only the status of Item 10 changed to "Completed," while the status of Item 20 remained "Released."

ItemParentServiceOrderItemproductitem categorystatus
10 ASVB4Completed
2010BSVS4Released
     

How do I change the status of Item 20 to "Completed"?

Soma

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content:
It was a pleasure.
I learned a lot on this challenge.
And perhaps most important, as an ABAP platform developer, I now know where to find the cap documentation in case I would need it 🙂

entry.title: #CoE#TFAL How to Handle Mass Upload of Transactions for One-Time Vendors in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), organizations often need to post multiple transactions (such as invoices) for one-time vendors. Unlike regular vendor master records, one-time vendors are designed for occasional use and require minimal master data.
 
As of today, SAP S/4HANA Cloud does not provide an out-of-the-box app or feature specifically designed for:
  • Bulk upload of transactions exclusively for one-time vendors
  • Dedicated mass processing tailored to one-time vendor workflows

Standard mass upload tools in SAP are primarily designed around:

  • Managed vendor master records
  • Structured master data governance
Have you implemented mass uploads for one-time vendors differently?
Feel free to share your approach or insights in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Hello - posting here in case anyone has faced/is facing the same issue we are. I'm currently at a loss as to how to proceed.

Currently our Mail Server config within IAS is setup as a Custom Mail Server for the purposes of managing the registrations of External Learning users. 

As you can see, we've tried to be as generic as possible in our setup given ONB will soon be moving to IAS. Note a few th ings:

  • our reply to of akosupport@ is designed for the 'External Learning' process. It does not work for our Onboarding process.
  • our from is gesapias@ , made to be as generic as possible. Imagine as a new starter of a company, receiving an email from gesapias@ instead of HR@. What an absolutely garbage onboarding experience.  

Given the above are tenant-level settings, I'm struggling to find a way to separate the 'External Learner' process from the 'External Onboardee' process; as they are very separate functions of the business and should not be subjected to the same interaction method.

With ONB IAS being a universal change come November 2026, how are people planning to address this? 

Is there anyone here in the same situation who has already moved to ONB IAS and how did you overcome this?

entry.title: Installation Management Notification - SAP for Me (SmartRecruiters)
entry.content:
Can anybody guide me regarding the following.

Received an notification from SAP for Me regarding Installation Management Update. 

Follwoing installation were created:

SMARTRECRUITERS FOR SAP SFSF

entry.content: @Taesuk Does it require any CDS annotation for delta extraction ? Does the delta is log or trigger based ?

entry.title: Re: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehous...
entry.content: Hi Simon, Thank you for the resposne. Could you please clarify how you register the qRFC from /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST? Do you create a custom RFC-enabled function module and enqueue it with a WMTH* queue, or is there a standard EWM helper API for registering the queue?

entry.title: Unable to Capture PO Number from Success Message in Test Your Process Apps – SAP S/4 Public cloud l
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on the Test Your Process application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have recorded a custom process and I am not using the standard scripts provided by SAP.

As part of my automation, I am trying to capture the PO number from the success message generated after creating a Purchase Order. However, I am unable to capture only the PO number.

Below Possibilities were tried nothing has Worked 

Label 

Value

ITCI - Standard PO created under the number 4500000520

ITCI - Standard PO created under the number (.*)

Standard PO created under the number 4500000520

ITCI - Standard PO created under the number (*)

Error Log in PO Number Capturing Process

Variant Action Steps in PO Process

PR Success Log

 

PR Action Variants Steps

 

I was able to capture the PR number successfully in the PR process and bind it to a variable. I am using the captured PR number in the subsequent PO process, and that part is working fine.

Can Some Help on  this

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Sourcing and Procurement

entry.title: Re: How to Push Data from SAP Datasphere to SAP S/4 HANA...
entry.content: Yes, we can replicate data from DSP to on-prem HANA, provided a few prerequisites are in place: 1. Proper DSP-to-HANA connectivity 2. The connection must be configured for Replication Flows and Data Flows. ⦁ In the connection settings, you should see that Replication Flows are enabled with a description indicating that the connection supports adding a source or target object to a replication flow. ⦁ The connection should also be configured through Cloud Connector for secure on-prem access. 1. Correct HANA on-prem security roles 2. On the HANA side, the target schema/table must have the appropriate permissions assigned. ⦁ The technical user used by DSP should have authority to create, insert, update, and/or load data into the target schema or table, depending on whether the table already exists or needs to be created. In short: the connection settings show that DSP supports remote and replication use cases, so the integration is possible. The write-back capability depends on the correct HANA authorizations and the replication flow design.

entry.title: Re: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehous...
entry.content: Hi Simon, Thank you for the resposne. Could you please clarify how you register the qRFC from /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST? Do you create a custom RFC-enabled function module and enqueue it with a WMTH* queue, or is there a standard EWM helper API for registering the queue?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: How to create or reuse Replication Fl...
entry.content: Hey, In such case you have different Replication Flows and local tables created initially and then union the data for those two tables using graphical view and then use that graphical view everywhere else. This will save you from data mix and also you have one union data set. Just a note: When you move to production as you will have one connection it is redundant to have two local tables created and merged into one. This is more of a solutioning / work around for the QA

entry.title: Purchase Requisition Missing in “Process Purchase Requisitions”? Here’s the Reason (and Fix)
entry.content:
Quick Solution

If a Purchase Requisition is missing in the “Process Purchase Requisitions” app,

👉   check the Close Indicator.

If it is set, the PR is treated as closed and will not appear in the app.

✔️   Fix: Remove the Close Indicator.

The Issue

A Purchase Requisition with status "Approved"
❌   is not visible in Process Purchase Requisitions
✅   is visible in Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional

According to 2856335 - Purchase Requisition List on Process Purchase Requisitions App - SAP for Me all checks have been performed and it is confirmed that the PR is:

  • Released 
  • Not on hold 
  • No follow-on documents 

Yet, it is still missing from Process Purchase Requisitions.

Root Cause

The Close Indicator was set.

This means:

  • PR is marked as completed
  • No further processing is expected
  • Therefore, it is excluded from the app Process Purchase Requisitions

Solution

  • Open the Purchase Requisition
  • Remove the Close Indicator

✔️   PR will appear again in Process Purchase Requisitions

Kerisha Lawrence

Public Cloud | IAM, Procurement, Post Go-Live

https://www.linkedin.com/in/kerisha-lawrence

entry.title: Re: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehous...
entry.content: You can use standard function module /SCWM/TO_CREATE_MOVE_HU. To call it via qRFC you have to call the function module TRFC_SET_QIN_PROPERTIES with parameter qin_name just before. The parameter can be filled with WMTH + Destination HU

entry.title: Need to update the Depreciation Type for multiple Fixed Assets using DTW.
entry.content:
SAP provides the ItemDepreciationParameter (ITM7) template, but I cannot find the corresponding object in DTW during Data Update to upload this template.

Has anyone successfully updated the Depreciation Type for existing Asset Master Data using DTW? If yes, where is the import option located, or is there any alternative supported method?

entry.title: Re: Badi for the Stock Information message at PR level
entry.content: 2.ME51N we are using and for the Approval we are using standard my inbox fiori app

entry.title: Issue in standard FM SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT
entry.content:
Issue in standard FM SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT. as per the standard FM, we have entries till 4096.   

  TABLES: marm.

  TYPE-POOLS:truxs.
  DATA:itab1 TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
  DATA:wa_itab1(4096) TYPE c

Now we are using the text values of about 9000 entries. Please suggest to provide any solution for this, how we can add this values upto 9000 entries.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content: @Maria_Radde Any updates on this?

entry.title: Re: Purchase Request – additional expenses (freight) cann...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

can you please specify which version are you exactly using? This is confirmed to work on FP2111 and newer.

I use SL for posting DocumentAdditionalExpenses (we have a scenario on Integration Framework for posting various documents) and it works fine, the only difference is that I do not send TaxCode in the array. That property is defined by the rules defined in Tax Determination, so maybe you can first try removing that from your payload.

 

EDIT: I only now realized you asked specifically for Purchase Request. No, you cannot add Additional Expenses to that document, as this is only meant to be an internal request to the Procurement team to find a suitable Vendor. 

 

BR,

Matija

entry.title: KEPM、計画データ入力の統合Excel有効でマクロエラー発生
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になります。MED三浦です。

KEPM、計画レベル＞計画パッケージ＞計画データ入力の統合Excel＝有効で、マクロエラーが発生する

entry.title: Session management and custom Field mappings in CDC
entry.content:
I have couple of questions where I am having doubts about.

  1. When we integrate the cdc with commerce, then you discussed that we have session management. So in BO we can configure the session and do the setup for session. Question here is:   
    1. If I give session to be led by CDC then does that mean that if time out from cdc is done then it will logout the session from CDC as well as Commerce? How does the commerce know that cdc session has expires so we have to invalidate the hybris session?
    2. Will there be any kind of token which needs to be invalidated from both cdc or commerce side?
  2. How does custom field mapping works? You asked me to mail you so that you can send me the link. Please let me know.

  3. What does 3600s mean? which we give while installing the cdc libraries in Spartacus. Does this mean that my session time is 1hr and after that my user will be logged out? What does it signify in terms of Session expiration. Is session management and this is related?

entry.title: Re: How to Push Data from SAP Datasphere to SAP S/4 HANA...
entry.content:
Yes, we can replicate data from DSP to on-prem HANA, provided a few prerequisites are in place:

  1. Proper DSP-to-HANA connectivity
  2. The connection must be configured for Replication Flows and Data Flows.
  • In the connection settings, you should see that Replication Flows are enabled with a description indicating that the connection supports adding a source or target object to a replication flow.
  • The connection should also be configured through Cloud Connector for secure on-prem access.
  1. Correct HANA on-prem security roles
  2. On the HANA side, the target schema/table must have the appropriate permissions assigned.
  • The technical user used by DSP should have authority to create, insert, update, and/or load data into the target schema or table, depending on whether the table already exists or needs to be created.

In short: the connection settings show that DSP supports remote and replication use cases, so the integration is possible. The write-back capability depends on the correct HANA authorizations and the replication flow design.

entry.title: Re: Translate FIORI tile's Title/Group Name
entry.content:
Hello

Could you please clean up the cache as per 

2319491        How to clean up the cache after applying changes that affect SAP Fiori apps

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002319491

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: How to create or reuse Replication Fl...
entry.content: Hey, In such case you can have different Replication Flows and local tables created initially and then union the data for those two tables using graphical view and then use that graphical view everywhere else. This will save you from data mix and also you will have one union data set. Just a note:  When you move to production as you will have one connection it is redundant to have two local tables created and merged into one. This is more of a work around for the QA

entry.title: Re: Set up your SAP S/4HANA system for EAM Inspection Checklists
entry.content:
Inspection points with maintenance task lists are used in the classic concept of calibration, but not in the new concept.

Please, see SAP notes:
3421917 - Inspection checklists and calibration
3421907 - EAM Checklists: Data model and use of Inspection Points

For simple checks at the start of work order that have no relation to technical object(s), I would recommend an initial operation with text instructions.

entry.title: Re: \/N/UI2/FLPD_CUST is not opening the launchpad design...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please provide HTTP Trace from Fiori Launchpad so we can analyze this issue better. To get the correct HTTP Trace please proceed with the following:

- Open your browser

- Open Fiori Launchpad logon screen

- Open Developer tools (press the key 12 on the keyboard)

- Log on to Fiori with developer tools opened

- Wait until all information gets loaded

- Select Network tab

- Right click on any request and save all as HAR with content

- Attach to the incident

- Go to console tab

- Right click and save

If it's not possible to open developer tools before you log on to Fiori, please logon, then open developer tools and refresh the page, then proceed from step 5 onwards.

Here is a KBA related to HTTP trace https://me.sap.com/notes/0002505725

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: Re: How to display Medium or Long Text instead of 15-char...
entry.content:
Hi Jared,

Yes it isn't the most friendly of solution from SAP in this regard.

In Fiori report 'Group Data Analysis' you can change to Long Text by right click and select Display -> Description. It will reset though the next time you open the report. The only method I have found here is to save the reports as bookmarks. Views will still revert to the standard.

In Group Review Booklet you change the Layout and select 'Display' -> 'Text Type' ->'Long Text'. This you can save as a View and it keeps the long description.

Br

Martin Jul

entry.title: Re: Alignment issue for webdynpro application when launch...
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please provide more detailed information about the query?

How to reproduce the issue.

Is there any error message there.

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: Re: How to reset approval status of sales order when it's...
entry.content: Approval status can be revoked via VA03 --> Edit --> Additional Functions --> Withdraw Approval Request

entry.title: Issue in PS Module - Billing of activities with quantum in 2 places after decimal
entry.content:
With the change in business process, a new billing activity has been added and its quantity has been defined up to two decimal places. For example, Geological Mapping – 10.85 SKM.

However, while entering the same in the PS module and during confirmation, the system is automatically rounding it to one decimal place, resulting in 10.90 SKM. This is not correct and is creating a discrepancy in the recorded quantity.

Kindly let us know for the resolution to get two decimal place in SKM measurement.

Lily

entry.title: Re: REFX - Flexible Real Estate books/Training?
entry.content: Hi, there is no traditional RE-FX learning material. The closest current learning option on the subject of real estate management is this learning journey:https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/describing-contracts-for-real-estate-management

entry.title: Re: Could be eslint-plugin-fiori-custom updated to newer...
entry.content:
Hey Jakub,

please use the new https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools instead. It comes with all the rules the old eslint-plugin-fiori-custom had but it works with eslint 10.

Please use the Application Info page of the SAP Fiori tools VScode extension (or in BAS) to convert.

There's also a cli command to convert (see https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/tree/main/packages/create#convert-eslint-config), but adding the new plugin from scratch gets you a better eslint config in my opinion (there's a cli command for that as well https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/tree/main/packages/create#add-eslint-config).

Cheers

Dominik (Fiori tools team)

entry.title: Re: QPR4 - is not displaying WorkCentre in the Output
entry.content:
Dear customer,

The QPR4 work center should be populated from table QPRS (sample master) - field LABOR (Work Center).

Please check if the work center value (QPRS-LABOR) exists for the sample.

 

Best regards

Lily

entry.title: Re: Blog#2: How Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD) can be integrated into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @pbalkrishnan, that's actually one of the use cases of Cross-Landscape-Distribution (XLD) . You can define a list of customizing tables (down to the key) that are relevant for the synchronization between S/4HANA and MDG.  It is very similar to what you might know from SAP Solution Manager.

And the XLD solution that you see in the video is part of pony!

entry.title: Re: Blog#1: Finally it is here - How "pony" brings custom fields to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Hi @Neha_Singh601, you need to deploy pony in your BTP subaccount and connected it with destinations to your SAP Cloud ALM tenant and your IAS.

entry.title: Re: How to handle Data Attributes Color and Volume
entry.content:
When the exact color is not available in the code list, the customer should choose the closest match - for lilac or purple that would be either RED or BLUE, depending on the nuance of the color. Leaving the color empty (null) may lead to wrong report fraction assignment.

Most regulations check for the most frequently used colors:

- Clear (natural) for all plastics

- Light Blue (LBL) for PET

- Green / Brown more often for glass, less often for plastic

- Black (for plastic and glass) - usually results in a penalty

Customers can open feature request for adding more colors or vote on existing requests for more colors.

Volume is required for hollow body containers - Bottles (BTL), Jars (JAR), Cans (CAN), Brick (BRK), Flask (FLA), 

It may be required for some other packaging types that could hold liquids or gases, like IBC, Tub (TUB), Tube (TBE), Aerosol container (GAC), Buckets (BCK), Cup (CUP) but these are seen less often in reports.

The generic container type, CNR, should also have volume, because some reports include CNR with the hollow-body containers.

We haven't seen a case where volume would be required for packaging that is "shallow" and rarely holds liquids - like TRY, TBW, Cage, Box, etc.

Due to EU Single-use plastic directive (SUP) all EU countries require the volume for beverage bottles and cups for beverages.

Customers should put the volume of the product that's inside the container, as it's printed on the label.

Obviously a bottle for 1.5L of beverage needs to have volume a little more than 1.5L, because of the empty space, but from our experience, PROs expect to see 1.5L in this case.

In the unlikely event, when the container has a lot of empty space (e.g 1L liquid in 3L container), the company should request guidance from the PRO how to maintain volume in this case.

entry.title: Re: Context Aware Data Action Scoping
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI Workspace: Features You Need to Know
entry.content: Good one 👍

entry.title: Flexible Workflow – Multiple Workflow Determination for Purchasing Group and Material Group
entry.content:
Our business requirement is to maintain two different approval groups:

* Approval Group 1: Based on Purchasing Group (e.g., VMU) with Approver 1 and Approver 2.
* Approval Group 2: Based on Material Group with Approver 3 and Approver 4.

For specific Material Groups, the workflow should be routed only to the Material Group approvers (Approver 3 and Approver 4). The Purchasing Group workflow should not be triggered for these Material Groups.

However, when we configure both workflows, both are triggered because the Purchasing Group condition is also satisfied.

entry.title: Re: Understanding HANA memory related license utilization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear @carlos_junior3 ,

Claudia's blog is specifically covering the License calculation in SAP HANA S/4HANA Public Cloud (Also known as SAP Cloud ERP). The monitoring view you are referring to are not relevant for this product. 

When it comes to setting the PAL and License, I believe it will very much depends on the SAP product you are running on top of HANA. But again this is a topic that is completely outside of this blog's scope. 

Clément Mugner

entry.title: Re: MDK app button bound to key
entry.content: MDK supports metadata-driven app customization and rule binding for UI elements such as buttons. do you have an example of this?

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content: Hello. I just checked in IP03 and the Object list Item tab has no entries. I even tried checking the change documents but nothing there as well.

entry.title: Re: Decimal of USD in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Moon3,

This is standard system behavior. The Amount field in Manage Purchasing Info Records only allows a maximum of 2 decimal places, so a value such as 0.025 PLN / PC cannot be maintained directly and will be rounded. SAP confirms this limitation for the app and also mentions changing the price unit of measure as one of the options to avoid the issue

More details below:

SAP KBA 3598661 - Limitation of Decimal Places in Amount Field in App Manage Purchasing Info Records

The recommended approach is to maintain the same commercial price with a larger price unit, for example:

0.025 PLN / 1 PC

should be maintained as:

25.00 PLN / 1000 PC

or

2.50 PLN / 100 PC

This keeps the same price mathematically, but avoids entering more than 2 decimals in the currency amount field

More details below:

SAP also confirms that the per-unit price in Purchase Orders is restricted to 2 decimal places and that higher precision is currently not available in the standard system

SAP KBA 3733837 - Purchase Order Pricing Precision Limited to 2 Decimal Places

It is also not possible in Public Cloud to change the standard decimal-place setting for currency amounts, for example changing PLN from 2 decimals to 3 or more decimals

More details below:

SAP KBA 3632144 - Limitation of extending Decimal Places for Currency Amounts in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

So for this case, please maintain the purchasing price using a higher price unit for example 25.00 PLN per 1000 PC and validate the result in the PO, goods receipt and supplier invoice process. Please also note that final document amounts in PLN will still be rounded according to the standard PLN currency decimals, so very small quantities may still lead to rounding differences

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content: Hello. I just tried checking in IP03. The Object list item tab is empty. There are no change documents as well.

entry.title: Recreating Excel‑Like Heat Maps in SAP Analytics Cloud Using Dynamic User‑Driven Thresholds
entry.content:
If you’ve worked with Excel heat maps, you already know how effortless it feels.

Just select your data → apply Conditional Formatting → Color Scales, and instantly you get a beautiful gradient showing highs, lows, and patterns. The colors automatically react to the values simple, intuitive, and powerful.

Excel :- 

But when you try to replicate the same behavior in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), things start to get interesting.

The Challenge in SAC

 In SAC, you can create heat maps and apply conditional formatting using thresholds. However:

Heat map charts rely on a single measure for color intensity
Conditional formatting in tables works with:
      -  Number ranges (static) OR
     -   Comparison to another measure (dynamic)
The real limitation comes when:  You want the gradient to adapt dynamically based on user-defined thresholds, like min/mid/max inputs similar to Excel flexibility.

Out-of-the-box, SAC doesn’t directly provide this level of interactive gradient control in a native heat map.

 So the question becomes:

Can we mimic Excel’s behavior using SAC building blocks?

Yes, and that’s exactly what this solution does.

 Solution Overview

 Instead of relying on the native SAC heat map, the approach shifts to:

          Using a Table + Calculated Measure + Conditional Formatting

This gives us full control over how colors are applied

 Step 1: Design the Data Structure

In the main SAC table:

Measure X → Holds the actual values (the data you want to visualize)
Values are stored at specific intersections (e.g., based on dimensions like Bay, Post, etc.)
Additionally, create 3 input measures:

  • Minimum
  • Midpoint
  • Maximum

These are not part of the main data grid, but stored at separate intersections where users can input values

Step 2: Enable User Input for Thresholds

Users enter:

  •  Minimum threshold
  •  Midpoint threshold
  •  Maximum threshold

This allows complete flexibility; users can dynamically control how the gradient behaves based on their scenario.

Step 3: Create the Logic – “XColorScore”

1) Created a Restricted Measure "RM-X" restricted  to its intersection at a a story level 

2) Created 3 Restricetd Measures one for each Measure restricted to its intersection at a story level

  •  Minimum (RM - Min)
  •  Maximum (RM - Max)
  •  Midpoint (RM - Mid)

3) Create a calculated measure (XColorScore) that:

Compares each Measure X value against Minimum, Midpoint, and Maximum

Example logic (conceptually):

If X <= Minimum → assign lower score
If X ≈ Midpoint → assign medium score
If X >= Maximum → assign higher score
Essentially, you convert your raw values into a color scoring scale.

This follows the SAC best practice of deriving a numeric output to drive conditional formatting

 

Step 4: Apply Conditional Formatting

Now comes the clever part.

In SAC:

  • Select Measure X 
  • Go to Conditional Formatting (Thresholds)
          Choose:  Compare To → Measure 
                            Comparison Measure → XColorScore

Since thresholds in SAC can compare one measure against another, this enables dynamic, data-driven coloring logic instead of static ranges.

 

Step 5: Achieve the Gradient Effect

By defining appropriate ranges in the threshold:

Green → lower values
Yellow → mid-range
Red → higher values
You effectively simulate:

  •  A heat map
  • A gradient scale
  • Based on user inputs


Why This Approach Works

This design leverages three powerful SAC concepts:

Separation of Data & Logic
→ Raw data (X) vs Colour logic (XColorScore)
User-driven input
→ Thresholds are not hardcoded
Measure-to-measure comparison
→ Unlocks dynamic conditional formatting

 

 Key Takeaways

Native SAC heat maps are great, but not always flexible enough
Conditional formatting with measure comparison is the real game changer
By introducing a scoring layer, you can simulate Excel-like gradients
Most importantly:  You give control back to the business user

Closing Thoughts

Moving from Excel to SAC often exposes these small gaps in flexibility.

But with a bit of creative modelling, SAC can go beyond Excel,especially when you combine:

  • Planning input
  •  Calculated measures
  •  Conditional formatting

This solution is a perfect example of that.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey, thanks a lot for this guide—it’s really helpful.

Even though we’ve uploaded the root certificate,


we’re still getting an error message stating that the certificate is invalid.


I did notice, however, that our certificate has a different name than the ones listed in the documentation. Instead of "DigiCert Global Root CA," our root certificate is named "Digital Guardian, Inc."

Do you have any tips on how to find the cause of the problem, or at least narrow it down?

entry.title: Re: Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standar...
entry.content: The issue is not with the account group config itself, but with missing credit management role/data for the domestic BP.  MKK is the role category for Customer Credit Management in SAP S/4HANA. This means the BP used for the Domestic Account Group (ZDOM) is missing the Credit Management role (UKM000 / FLCUCR) or it is not properly maintained for the company code/plant you are working with. For ZFRN, the BP likely has the correct roles assigned (Customer role + Credit Management role), so the sales order creation succeeds.

entry.title: Vendor Evaluation with AI Prompt Engineering in SAP AI
entry.content:
By Satish Vallabhaneni and Raunak Bhatia

Introduction:

 In today’s fast-paced procurement landscape, every delayed vendor onboarding costs your business time and money. While traditional automation handles structured data well, managing unstructured supplier emails, diverse document formats, and initial risk checks still requires heavy manual effort. Enter the Prompt Editor within the SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub. By leveraging this powerful tool, you can build, test, and deploy tailored AI prompts that automatically extract vendor details, draft introductory emails, and trigger onboarding workflows in seconds. In this blog post, we will walk through how to utilize the Prompt Editor to orchestrate a seamless, low-effort vendor onboarding process that integrates directly with your existing SAP landscape.

 

Procurement teams spend enormous time manually reviewing vendor documents — financial statements, compliance certificates, insurance documents, reference letters — before onboarding a new supplier or doing annual risk reviews. In large enterprises this can take 3–7 days per vendor, and with hundreds of vendors onboarded annually, the cumulative cost is massive.

With the Prompt Editor's multi-modal capability (text + document upload), you can demo a live scenario where a raw vendor document goes in and a structured risk assessment narrative comes out in seconds — something that would normally take a sourcing analyst half a day.

Quantifiable business impact to present:

  • Vendor onboarding cycle time reduced from days to hours
  • Risk assessment cost per vendor reduced by 60–75%
  • Consistent, auditable risk scoring (removes human subjectivity)
  • Compliance team bandwidth freed for exception handling only

 

Execution:

Step-by-Step: How to Demo This in SAP AI Launchpad Prompt Editor

Step 1 — Open Prompt Editor
Navigate to SAP AI Launchpad → Generative AI Hub → Prompt Editor

 

 

Step 2 — Select a Multi-modal Model
From the Model Library, select a model that supports document/image input such as Gemini 2.5 Create a deployment if not already active. This is critical because you'll be uploading vendor documents directly.

 

 

 

Step 3 — Prepare a Sample Vendor Document
Use a dummy or anonymized vendor document — ideally a 1–2 page PDF containing:

  • Company registration details
  • Annual turnover figures
  • Compliance certifications (ISO, SOC2 etc.)
  • Bank/financial reference

 

Sample document in PDF format

 

 

 

Step 4 — Set the System Prompt
In the System prompt section of the editor, define the AI's role clearly:

"You are a Procurement Risk Analyst at a large enterprise. When given a vendor document, you will extract key risk indicators and produce a structured vendor risk summary with: (1) Vendor Profile, (2) Financial Risk Rating (Low/Medium/High), (3) Compliance Status, (4) Key Red Flags if any, (5) Recommended Next Action. Keep the output concise and suitable for a Procurement Manager review."

 

 

Step 5 — Upload the Vendor Document
Use the Upload Image/Document button in the Prompt Editor interface, attach your sample vendor PDF, and add a simple user message:

"Please analyse this vendor document and generate the risk narrative."

 

 

Step 6 — Tune Parameters

  • Set Temperature to 0.1–0.2 for consistent, factual output
  • Set Max Tokens to 500–800 to keep the narrative concise but complete

Step 7 — Run and Review Output
Hit Run and show stakeholders the structured risk narrative generated in seconds. Compare it side-by-side with how long a manual review would take.

 

 

 

Step 8 — Test with Contrast Scenarios
Run the same prompt against two vendor documents — one clean, one with a red flag (e.g., missing certification, low turnover). Show how the AI correctly differentiates risk levels. This is the moment that impresses the room.

 

 

 

 

Step 9 — Save to Prompt Registry
Save the validated prompt as a versioned template in Prompt Management, so Procurement, Legal, and Compliance teams all use the same standardised assessment logic — removing inconsistency across analysts.

Step 10 — Present the ROI
Build a simple metric: if the company onboards 300 vendors/year, and each manual review takes 4 hours at an analyst cost of ₹800/hour, that's ₹9.6 lakhs per year in manual effort — most of which gets eliminated. Add speed-to-onboard improvement and you have a compelling CFO-ready number.

 

Closing:

Streamlining your vendor onboarding process does not require a complete overhaul of your procurement systems; it simply requires adding a layer of intelligent automation. By mastering the Prompt Editor in the SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub, you transform a traditionally slow, manual chore into an instant, automated workflow. This not only cuts down on administrative errors but also allows your procurement teams to focus on strategic supplier relationships rather than data entry. As you take your next steps into generative AI, try building a basic extraction prompt using your own vendor templates—you will be amazed at how quickly you can turn raw supplier data into a ready-to-use business asset.

entry.title: Re: Unabel to do Partial capitalization in KO02
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The error is coming because the settlement rule is maintained with settlement type FUL.

For FUL / Actual Settlement, SAP expects the total receiver percentage to be 100%. In your case, only 50% has been maintained, so the system is stopping you with the message:

“Total percent = 50% (Set. Type FUL / Version / Actual settlement)”

You may refer to this link for more information

This is standard behavior. SAP needs to know where the full amount should be settled. If only 50% is assigned to an asset, the remaining 50% has no receiver, so the settlement rule cannot be saved.

If you want to split the capitalization, you need to maintain another receiver line so that the total becomes 100%. For example:

Asset 1 = 50%
Asset 2 / Cost Center / other valid receiver = 50%

However, if your requirement is to capitalize only 50% now and keep the remaining 50% on AuC for later, then this should not be done by maintaining only 50% in the FUL rule. You may need to review the settlement design, such as periodic/preliminary settlement or the correct receiver setup, depending on your business process.

In short, for settlement type FUL, the total percentage must always be 100%. Partial capitalization does not mean that the FUL rule can be maintained with only 50%.

I hopes this help!

entry.title: Re: Billing Plan (Periodic)-SAP SD-Part1
entry.content: Very useful information...!👍
